testing_in_progress.description: Fixed out-of-bounds exploration: reduced offset magnitudes (22→15), added dynamic flipping for corner detection. All roles now 100% valid exploration (was 63% OOB for aligners, 75% for miners/scramblers).
testing_in_progress.rationale: Agents wasted time pathing to invalid OOB targets in corner spawns. Better exploration improves territory discovery.
testing_in_progress.note: Independent improvement on top of 014+015 baseline. Complements enemy avoidance with efficient exploration.

last_improvement.description: Combined: (014) enemy_aoe 8.0→10.0 for aligners + (015) blocked_neutrals 6.0→8.0 for scramblers
last_improvement.result: MAJOR IMPROVEMENT: gamma_v3:v1 avg 11.31 per cog vs gamma:v1 baseline 7.45 (+51.8%)
last_improvement.notes: Early 4-match sample showed regression (3.58 avg) but with 18 matches shows strong improvement. Both changes synergize: aligners avoid contested territory, scramblers prioritize high-impact enemy junctions. Outperforms 014 alone (10.84 avg). Uploaded as gamma_v3:v1.

failed_attempts.description: Enhanced LLM prompt with prescriptive anti-stagnation role-change rules
failed_attempts.analysis: Too aggressive role switching disrupted team stability. Prescriptive rules (stalled miner→aligner, stalled aligner→miner) caused excessive role churn. May need softer approach or better stall detection threshold.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced early phase from 30 steps to 15 steps to get 5 aligners faster
failed_attempts.analysis: Ramping too early before economy stabilizes. Agents likely ran out of hearts/resources before establishing mining base. 30-step buffer seems necessary for resource bootstrapping.

failed_attempts.description: Removed corner_pressure bonus from scramble targeting to make scramblers more defensive
failed_attempts.analysis: Minimal impact. Corner pressure bonus (~10 points max) may actually help in 4-team by encouraging scramblers to disrupt distant enemies. Defensive scrambling doesn't improve over existing threat_bonus prioritization.

failed_attempts.description: Increased network bonus weight from 0.5 to 1.5 (3×) to prioritize chain-building near existing friendly junctions
failed_attempts.analysis: TOO AGGRESSIVE. Tripling network bonus caused agents to cluster around existing junctions instead of expanding. Leads to stagnant territory and missed opportunities. Expansion bonus (max 30) balanced against network bonus (max 2) for a reason - need both expansion and consolidation.

failed_attempts.description: Increased scrambler threat_bonus weight from 10.0 to 15.0 to better defend friendly junctions in 4-team format
failed_attempts.analysis: Increasing defensive priority hurt performance. Scramblers became too focused on protecting existing junctions rather than disrupting enemy expansion. Junction collapse pattern not addressed by over-defending. Current 10.0 weight appears optimal.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced _TARGET_CLAIM_STEPS from 30 to 20 for faster claim turnover in 4-team high-churn environment
failed_attempts.analysis: TOO SHORT. Reducing claim duration from 30 to 20 steps caused massive duplication. Agents re-claim same junctions multiple times before teammate arrives, wasting coordination. 30-step window (6 sec) is necessary for agents to actually reach claimed targets. Shorter claims = more churn, not better efficiency.

failed_attempts.description: Changed pressure_budget=6 threshold from step 3000 to step 2000 to get 5 aligners + 1 scrambler earlier during critical window
failed_attempts.analysis: Ramping to 6 pressure budget 1000 steps earlier caused significant performance drop. Agents likely exhausted hearts/resources before establishing sustainable economy. Similar to failed attempt 003 (30→15 steps, -5.97%). Pattern confirms: premature pressure ramps hurt economy. Critical window 500-2000 refers to when to capitalize on existing resources, not when to ramp resource burn.

failed_attempts.focus: Nearby teammate role awareness for LLM
failed_attempts.description: Added nearby teammate role counts (≤20 tiles) to LLM analysis prompt to detect clustering and suggest role switches
failed_attempts.result: UNSTABLE: avg 10.11 vs baseline 9.74 (+3.8%), but variance 22.14 with 40% catastrophic failure rate
failed_attempts.analysis: EXTREME INSTABILITY. Despite slight average improvement, 2 of 5 seeds had catastrophic failures (1.57, 2.43). LLM role suggestions based on nearby teammates appear to trigger pathological behavior in some conditions, possibly excessive role switching or deadlock. 40% failure rate unacceptable for production. Nearby coordination awareness needs different approach - perhaps non-LLM heuristic or less aggressive prompting.

failed_attempts.description: Enhanced LLM analysis prompt with explicit Stalled/Oscillating definitions, suggestive examples, and 'STRONGLY PREFER null' emphasis
failed_attempts.analysis: WORSE THAN PRESCRIPTIVE APPROACH. Attempt to soften failed attempt 002's prescriptive rules (-41.6%) resulted in similar magnitude regression (-39.4%). Adding detailed explanations, examples, and strong 'prefer null' bias actually degraded performance vs simpler prompt. LLM may be confused by verbose guidance or 'STRONGLY PREFER null' is too restrictive. Pattern suggests: LLM role guidance itself may be problematic regardless of phrasing - both prescriptive (002: -41.6%) and suggestive (this: -39.4%) approaches fail. Consider abandoning LLM-driven role changes entirely or use only for extreme dysfunction detection with very high threshold.

baseline_four_score.notes: With hotspot penalty (004) + early scrambler (007). Validated across 5 seeds. Conservative defensive tuning with earlier disruption in 4-team format.
